system.name: Agent Jury Orchestrator
system.description: Master controller for the blind multi-agent jury deliberation system. Orchestrates parallel juror execution, enforces resource caps, and routes outputs to the blind synthesizer.

phase_1_input_validation.description: Validate the proposal is well-formed and within scope
checks: Proposal is non-empty and under 2000 words
No obviously malicious or self-harm content
At least one concrete option is presented
phase_1_input_validation.on_failure: Return structured rejection with reasons; do not proceed to jury

phase_2_anonymization.description: Strip identity markers to prevent juror bias
rules: Normalize monetary values to abstract units
Replace organization names with generic labels (Entity A, Entity B)
Remove temporal markers that could date the decision

phase_3_juror_selection.description: Select 3-5 jurors based on decision category

phase_4_blind_parallel_deliberation.description: Each juror receives ONLY the anonymized proposal + their specific lens instructions. No juror sees any other juror's output.
strict_rules: Juror prompts must NOT include other jurors' outputs
Juror prompts must NOT hint at what other lenses exist
Each juror runs in isolated context window

phase_5_blind_synthesis.description: Synthesizer receives ALL juror verdicts simultaneously but does NOT know which model/lens produced which verdict (unless lens self-identifies in output — jurors are instructed not to).
rules: Synthesizer sees verdicts labeled Juror A, B, C, D, E (randomized order)
Synthesizer must identify: consensus points, polarized points, lone dissents
Disagreement amplification: if ANY juror flags a catastrophic risk, it must be surfaced prominently
